text.title: Kanban and SAFe Ceremonies Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the Kanban and SAFe Ceremonies methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Ceremony set MUST be one of {kanban-lightweight, kanban-cadenced, safe-essential, safe-full}; ad-hoc subsets are rejected.
rule.rationale: named sets keep teams comparable; ad-hoc subsets prevent program-level reporting.

rule.statement: Every flow stage MUST have an explicit WIP limit; stages without WIP limits collapse into a wish list.
rule.rationale: missing WIP limits are the #1 reason Kanban transformations stall.

rule.statement: Replenishment (replacing 'Ready' items) MUST run on a fixed cadence (weekly or biweekly); ad-hoc replenishment is forbidden.
rule.rationale: ad-hoc replenishment makes flow metrics non-comparable.

rule.statement: SAFe PI planning MUST run on a fixed 8-12 week cadence with named RTE; missing PI planning windows are escalations to the program board.
rule.rationale: without locked PI planning windows the program loses its synchronisation backbone.

rule.statement: Flow metrics (lead time, cycle time, throughput, WIP) MUST be published per cadence; missing metrics are rejected.
rule.rationale: without metrics the cadence becomes ritual without signal.
